text: When you work with an API, you send HTTP requests to a server to get or send data. Most APIs use JSON, a simple text format that organises information clearly. Many APIs require authentication, such as an API key, to confirm you are allowed to access the data. While using an API, be aware of rate limits. These limits restrict how many requests you can make within a certain time. If you send too many, the server may block you temporarily. You should also handle errors properly. For example, if a request fails, you can check the error code returned and try again later. Good error handling makes your application more reliable.
